name: Fig (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw common fig, Ficus carica). concentration_mM assumes 100 g ~ 100 mL (water-rich matrix, density ~1).

components.name: water
components.evidence: matrix / solvent, ~79% w/w

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Southern-Mediterranean fig HPLC (Tunisian cvs, glucose 6.30 g/100g fw; tandfonline 10.1080/10942912.2011.642447) + review (fresh fig glucose ~5.8 g/100g fw)
components.evidence: CO-DOMINANT carbon source; glucose and fructose are the two principal free sugars, roughly equal, glucose slightly higher

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: Southern-Mediterranean fig HPLC (fructose ~5.10 g/100g fw; tandfonline 10.1080/10942912.2011.642447)
components.evidence: CO-DOMINANT carbon source; near-equal to glucose in fresh fig

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: minor relative to the reducing sugars; sucrose < glucose ~ fructose

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: present per FooDB / fig cell-wall galactans
components.evidence: not a major FREE sugar; occurs mainly bound in pectic galactans; unquantified as free monosaccharide

components.name: trehalose
components.source: Palmeira et al. 2019 Food Res Int, Portuguese fig (PMID 31732029) - trehalose detected among 4 free sugars
components.evidence: minor free sugar, detected but low; unquantified here

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: total reducing+non-reducing sugars; individual split (glucose~fructose>>sucrose) from HPLC literature above; cultivar/ripeness variation is large

components.name: starch
components.evidence: low in ripe fig (converted to free sugars during ripening); FooDB 'Starch' entry retained only as trace

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Southern-Mediterranean fresh-fig HPLC (citric 0.35 g/100g fw, major acid, ~3x malic; tandfonline 10.1080/10942912.2011.642447); Organic Acid Profiles in Fig Juice (citric ~66% of total acids)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid in most fresh-fig cultivars and a major secondary carbon/energy source; was MISSING from the FooDB profile

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Southern-Mediterranean fresh-fig HPLC (malic 0.13 g/100g fw; tandfonline 10.1080/10942912.2011.642447)
components.evidence: second organic acid (L-malate, already in FooDB list but unquantified); ~15-16% of total acidity

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Palmeira et al. 2019 (PMID 31732029): succinic most abundant acid in PULP (484 mg/100g dw); converted to ~0.10 g/100g fw at ~79% moisture
components.evidence: pulp-dominant TCA acid; note dw->fw conversion, so treat as approximate; was MISSING from FooDB profile

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Palmeira et al. 2019 (PMID 31732029) + Organic Acid Profiles in Fig Juice - quinic detected in fig fruit
components.evidence: genuine fig organic acid, present but not consistently quantified in fw; include for completeness; was MISSING from FooDB profile

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Palmeira et al. 2019 (PMID 31732029) - oxalic among 5 detected fig acids
components.evidence: minor/trace organic acid; unquantified in fw; was MISSING from FooDB profile

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: low-protein matrix

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: DOMINANT amino acid (aspartate/asparagine pool)

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: second-most-abundant amino acid

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: principal mineral

components.name: iron
components.evidence: consolidates the redundant FooDB iron entries (Iron / Iron Fe3+ / Fe2+ mitochondria are BiGG compartment artifacts of one nutrient)

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: trace

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: also a mild organic acid; listed once here

components.name: choline
components.evidence: corresponds to FooDB 'Choline'

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: corresponds to FooDB 'Nicotinate'

components.name: thiamin (B1)
components.evidence: corresponds to FooDB 'Thiamine hydrochloride'

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: present per FooDB; USDA does not quantify
components.evidence: trace, unquantified

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: absent (plant food); flag if a model requires B12 supplementation

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: corresponds to FooDB 'Retinol' (as RAE from provitamin A)

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: main provitamin-A carotenoid

components.name: alpha-tocopherol (vitamin E)
components.evidence: gamma- and delta-tocopherol also present (FooDB 'Gamma-Tocopherol','d-Tocopherol') but unquantified in USDA

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: corresponds to FooDB 'Phytomenadione'

components.name: betaine (glycine betaine)
components.source: present per FooDB 'Glycine betaine'; not quantified in USDA figs
components.evidence: genuine trace osmolyte, unquantified

components.name: total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: fig is a very low-fat matrix; fatty acids are nutritionally minor for growth

components.name: total polyunsaturated fatty acids
components.evidence: dominant lipid class, mostly linoleic + alpha-linolenic acid

components.name: total monounsaturated fatty acids
components.evidence: mostly oleic acid (18:1 n-9)

components.name: total saturated fatty acids
components.evidence: mostly palmitic acid (16:0)

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.source: USDA FDC 173021 (dominant PUFA of the 0.145 g FAPU); FooDB '(Z,Z)-9,12-Octadecadienoic acid'
components.evidence: principal fatty acid; individual value bounded by FAPU total

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.source: USDA FDC 173021 (part of FAPU); FooDB 'alpha-Linolenic acid'
components.evidence: second PUFA; individual value bounded by FAPU total

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 173021 (main SFA of the 0.060 g FASAT)
components.evidence: principal saturated fatty acid; individual value bounded by FASAT total

components.name: medium-chain fatty acids (C6-C12: hexanoic/octanoic/decanoic/dodecanoic)
components.evidence: retained as trace only; negligible at fig's 0.30 g total fat and not growth-relevant; keep flagged rather than removed

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: includes pectin (galacturonan / 'Pectic acid' in FooDB) - partly fermentable by pectinolytic microbes; see keep_uncovered

remove.name: Gadoleic acid (20:1)
remove.reason: very-long-chain MUFA not a genuine fig-fruit lipid; FooDB predicted/noise, implausible at fig's 0.30 g total fat

remove.name: Erucic acid (22:1)
remove.reason: Brassica-type very-long-chain fatty acid, biologically implausible in fig fruit; FooDB predicted/noise

remove.name: Nonanoate (nonanoic acid, C9:0)
remove.reason: odd-chain fatty acid, not a measured fig-fruit lipid; FooDB predicted/noise

remove.name: Co2+ / Cobalt
remove.reason: ultratrace, non-nutritive at fig levels (no B12 in fig); FooDB noise

remove.name: Rubber, natural-smoked sheet and latex solids (Hevea brasiliensis) [uncovered]
remove.reason: nonsensical database artifact (a commodity rubber entry), not a fig constituent

remove.name: Pentosan polysulfate [uncovered]
remove.reason: semi-synthetic pharmaceutical drug, impossible endogenous fig constituent; database artifact

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine [uncovered]
remove.reason: microbial-fermentation food preservative, not a fig constituent; database artifact

remove.name: (+/-)-erythro-Isoleucine [uncovered]
remove.reason: racemic/diastereomer artifact duplicate of L-isoleucine already in the amino-acid panel

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria (duplicate entries)
remove.reason: BiGG compartment/redox artifacts of a single nutrient (iron); consolidated into one 'iron' component (0.37 mg/100g)

missing_added: citric acid (0.35 g/100g - DOMINANT organic acid, was absent from FooDB profile)
succinic acid (~0.10 g/100g fw - pulp-dominant TCA acid, was absent)
quantified L-malate (0.13 g/100g - was present but unquantified)
quantified individual sugars: D-glucose ~6.0, D-fructose ~5.5, sucrose ~1.4 g/100g fw (co-dominant glucose~fructose)
keep_uncovered: psoralen, bergapten (5-MOP), (R)-oxypeucedanin (genuine fig furanocoumarins, concentrated in skin/latex)
cyanidin and pelargonidin anthocyanidins; cyanidin-3-rhamnoglucoside underlies the dark-fig skin colour
catechin / alpha-catechin, procyanidin B3 (flavan-3-ols / condensed tannins)
pectin / pectic acid (galacturonan - partly fermentable polysaccharide, no single BiGG met)
invert sugar (i.e. the glucose+fructose already quantified as free sugars above)
boron (genuine trace plant mineral, no core BiGG growth role)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 173021 (SR Legacy, Figs, raw) - full proximate/mineral/vitamin/amino-acid/fatty-acid-class panel
Southern-Mediterranean fresh-fig HPLC study (Tunisian cultivars), Food Sci - doi:10.1080/10942912.2011.642447 (glucose 6.30, fructose 5.10 g/100g fw; citric 0.35, malic 0.13 g/100g fw)

Palmeira, Barros, Ferreira et al. 2019, Food Research International - Portuguese common fig, PMID 31732029 (4 free sugars incl. trehalose; 5 organic acids oxalic/quinic/malic/citric/succinic; succinic dominant in pulp 484 mg/100g dw)
Organic Acid Profiles in the Juice of Fig Fruits (citric ~66%, malic ~16%, gluconic ~12% of total acids)
Alzahrani 2024 review, Food Safety and Health doi:10.1002/fsh3.12034 (fresh fig sugar ranges)
FooDB FOOD00081 (source of the original component list)
notes: Dominant carbon sources are the co-equal free reducing sugars glucose (~6.0 g/100g) ~ fructose (~5.5), with minor sucrose (~1.4); total sugars ~16.3 g. The major organic acid (and key secondary energy source), ABSENT from the original FooDB profile, is citric acid (~0.35 g/100g), with malic (~0.13) and pulp-dominant succinic (~0.10) secondary. Fig is a low-protein (0.75 g), very-low-fat (0.30 g), K/citrate-rich mildly-acidic fruit medium; individual sugar and organic-acid amounts vary strongly with cultivar and ripeness (confidence medium for those). Removed FooDB artifacts: implausible very-long-chain/odd-chain fatty acids (gadoleic, erucic, nonanoate), a pharmaceutical (pentosan polysulfate), a preservative (epsilon-polylysine), a rubber-commodity entry, a duplicate amino-acid isomer, and redundant iron compartment entries. A metabolic model may need vitamin B12 supplemented (absent).
